title: Move Success Criteria to Work Item ownership
outOfScope: Success to Acceptance to Check execution-record traceability
Release, fixture, and plan-cleanup work items

sources.reason: Work Item 5 ownership and compatibility acceptance.

riskAssessment.reason: Active Work Items must resolve their own criteria while historic global declarations remain compatible.

executionDecision.reason: Work Item-owned criteria, compatibility fallback, and archive evidence are specified.

preReviewWarnings: Verify active Work Items prefer their own criteria and archived criteria remain discoverable.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: A Work Item-specific .success.json is preferred over the legacy global project declaration.
The global declaration remains a compatibility fallback for historic or unassigned Work Items.
Archiving a Work Item moves its success declaration with the Contract and Summary.
Tests and Trust Layer documentation cover active ownership, fallback, and archive behavior.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: Success Criteria currently resolve from one project-global file, so concurrent Work Items cannot own, archive, or independently verify their criteria.
constraints: Prefer Work Item-owned criteria without breaking historic global declarations.
Keep criteria schema validation strict and machine-readable.
Archive criteria together with Contract and Summary.
intent.rationale: Task-scoped criteria provide lifecycle isolation while an explicit fallback preserves compatibility for existing adopters.

rawUserRequest: 继续执行第 5 个工单：将 Success Criteria 从全局路径迁移到 Work Item 归属范围，支持隔离、归档和兼容。

declaredIntent.summary: Resolve and archive Success Criteria per Work Item with a legacy fallback.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Active Work Item has a valid task-owned success declaration
Active Work Item has no task-owned declaration but legacy global criteria exist
Archiving an active Work Item with task-owned criteria

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the complete sequential remediation, including governance policy calibration.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
